target.name: Clippership
target.founder: Nico Cymbalist, Luca Cymbalist, Kai Matsuka

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Clippership is a Seed-stage maritime technology company that has raised $5M+. The valuation framework relies on a limited comp universe of three private peers in ocean and maritime technology—Standard Subsea, NetworkOcean, and Albacore—with no public comparables available in this sector.

The stage-progression method, the sole applicable triangulation approach given data constraints, uses the $5M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage transitions. This yields an implied valuation of $12M. Sensitivity analysis around this central method—adjusting for execution risk and stage-specific variability—supports a range of $11M to $14M, with $12M as the median point estimate.

The analysis is materially constrained by reliance on a single method and sparse private comp data. The $5M raised proxy itself masks the company's actual post-money valuation from the most recent financing, which would improve precision. Without public market anchors or additional private transaction multiples, the $11M–$14M range should be treated as an initial framework pending refinement as the company approaches Series A and larger data sets become available.
